note: Award criteria, weights, thresholds, formatting and eligibility rules for MSCA Postdoctoral Fellowships. Sub-criteria wording follows the official REA evaluation form. Verify against the current Work Programme and call documents before relying on this for a live submission.

sub_criteria.name: Research objectives & beyond the state of the art
sub_criteria.text: Quality and pertinence of the project's research and innovation objectives (and the extent to which they are ambitious, and go beyond the state of the art).
required_elements: explicit description of the state of the art
statement of how the project goes beyond the state of the art

sub_criteria.name: Soundness of methodology
sub_criteria.text: Soundness of the proposed methodology, including interdisciplinary approaches, consideration of the gender dimension and other diversity aspects if relevant, and the quality of the open science practices.
required_elements: open science practices (open access, FAIR data, open data)

sub_criteria.name: Training & two-way transfer of knowledge
sub_criteria.text: Quality and appropriateness of the training and of the two-way transfer of knowledge between the researcher and the host.
required_elements: concrete training activities (scientific and transferable skills)
two-way transfer of knowledge researcher <-> host

sub_criteria.name: Supervision & integration in the host
sub_criteria.text: Quality of the supervision and of the integration in the team/institution.
required_elements: supervisor track record and time commitment
integration in the host team / institution

sub_criteria.name: Career perspectives & employability
sub_criteria.text: Credibility of the measures to enhance the career perspectives and employability of the researcher and contribution to his/her skills development.
required_elements: how the fellowship advances the researcher's career
target employment sectors after the fellowship

sub_criteria.name: Dissemination, exploitation & communication
sub_criteria.text: Suitability and quality of the measures to maximise expected outcomes and impacts, as set out in the dissemination and exploitation plan, including communication activities.

sub_criteria.name: Magnitude & importance of impact
sub_criteria.text: The magnitude and importance of the project's contribution to the expected scientific, societal and (where relevant) economic impacts.
required_elements: scale and significance of expected outcomes

criteria.name: Quality and Efficiency of the Implementation

sub_criteria.name: Work plan, effort & risk management
sub_criteria.text: Quality and effectiveness of the work plan, assessment of risks and appropriateness of the effort assigned to work packages.
required_elements: work packages with objectives, tasks and deliverables

sub_criteria.name: Host capacity & infrastructure
sub_criteria.text: Quality and capacity of the host (institutional environment, infrastructure) to host and support the action.

interpretation.label: Competitive / fundable
interpretation.note: In most panels successful proposals score ~90% or above. Strong across all three criteria.

interpretation.label: Strong but borderline
interpretation.note: Above threshold and well-written, but unlikely to be funded in a competitive panel without sharpening.

interpretation.label: Above threshold, not competitive
interpretation.note: Meets the formal 70% quality threshold but would not normally be funded.

interpretation.label: Below threshold
interpretation.note: Does not meet the 70% quality threshold; major rework needed.

part_b1_sections: 3. Quality and Efficiency of the Implementation
formatting.page_limit_note: Sections 1-3 of Part B1 must fit within 10 A4 pages. Excess pages are disregarded by evaluators.
formatting.font_body: Times New Roman (or Times / Nimbus Roman No. 9 L) 11pt minimum

reviewer_tips.tip: Top proposals score near-maximum on ALL three criteria. The 50/30/20 weighting does not mean you can neglect Implementation — a weak work plan sinks an otherwise excellent proposal.
An inadequate state-of-the-art is the single most common Excellence weakness: without it evaluators cannot judge novelty.
Implementation (20%) is the most frequently rushed section. Provide a real Gantt chart, work packages, milestones, deliverables and an honest risk table.
Applicants routinely fail to explain how the fellowship advances THEIR career. The Career Development Plan must be concrete and specific to the researcher.
The first page is a sales document — it must be clear, engaging and intriguing on first read because evaluators are busy.
Use the official evaluation form as a checklist while writing — mirror its language so evaluators can find every scored element.
reviewer_tips.source: REA evaluation form; Ten Simple Rules (PLOS)

reviewer_tips.tip: Address the gender dimension in the research CONTENT (not just gender balance in the team) wherever relevant, and justify if not relevant.
Open science is mandatory, not optional: commit to open access, a Data Management Plan and FAIR data.
Make the two-way transfer of knowledge explicit: what the researcher brings to the host AND what they gain.
Keep within 10 pages for sections 1-3; anything beyond is not read. Respect 11pt Times New Roman and 15mm margins or risk inadmissibility.

disclaimer: ClawBioCrop / MSCA-Reviewer is a research and educational tool. It does not represent the European Commission, REA or any evaluation panel, and its scores are heuristic estimates only — not official evaluation results. Always verify against the current MSCA Work Programme, Guide for Applicants and call-specific evaluation form before submitting.
